summary: Completed testing of orientation-based features: PageBanner component, Registration orientation selector, and backend API changes. All 10 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend UI testing confirmed all features working correctly. Fixed one backend bug in /api/auth/profile endpoint.

minor.issue: orientations parameter wasn't being parsed correctly as a list - FIXED by adding Query() annotation

critical_code_review_comments: PageBanner component correctly renders on PartnersPage with animated images and crossfade effect
PageBanner shows default/heterosexual banner for guests (not logged in)
PageBanner shows orientation-specific banners for logged-in users based on their orientations field
Registration page has all 4 orientation selector buttons (Heteroseksüel, Lezbiyen, Gay, Biseksüel) with proper data-testid attributes
User registration with orientation selection works correctly - orientations array is stored in database
Login endpoint returns user with orientations field
/api/auth/profile can update user orientations (fixed Query() annotation for list parameter)

test_credentials.test_users: Various test users created during testing with password Test123!

seed_data_creation: Multiple test users created with different orientations during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Orientation-based features fully tested and working: 1) PageBanner component on PartnersPage shows different banners based on user orientation - uses useAuth() hook to get user.orientations. 2) Registration page has orientation selector with 4 buttons (heterosexual, lesbian, gay, bisexual) - toggleOrientation function allows multiple selection. 3) Backend UserCreate model includes orientations: List[str] = []. 4) Backend UserResponse includes orientations field. 5) /api/auth/register accepts orientations array. 6) /api/auth/login returns user with orientations. 7) /api/auth/me returns orientations. 8) /api/auth/profile can update orientations (fixed Query() annotation).

page_banner_component.orientation_specific_banners: PASS - shows different images based on user.orientations[0]
page_banner_component.headlines_change: PASS - different headlines for different orientations

registration_orientation_selector.registration_with_orientation: PASS - user created with orientations array

backend_api.profile_update_endpoint: PUT /api/auth/profile - PASS (can update orientations, fixed Query() annotation)

bug_fix_applied.issue: orientations: Optional[List[str]] = None wasn't parsing query params correctly
bug_fix_applied.fix: Changed to orientations: Optional[List[str]] = Query(None) to properly parse list query parameters
